## Claude Code Desktop Session — RHO Completion Review and Planning

### What happened
1. Glen asked for a full review of the RHO harness self-learning system
2. Comprehensive audit: read every file in `.claude/harness/`, full spec, cadence prompt. Identified 8 implementation areas with v1 gaps.
3. Wrote completion design spec (`docs/specs/2026-06-17-rho-completion-design.md`), Glen approved
4. Phase 1 plan written, went through 5 rounds of Codex adversarial review:
   - Rev 1 → 18 findings (4 CRITICAL). Core issue: risk classifier is path-only, can't support constraints
   - Rev 2 → 15 resolved, 9 new. Introduced forgeable --approved hash
   - Rev 3 → 15 resolved, 3 new (1 CRITICAL: forgeable hash)
   - Rev 3b → stale text scrub. Verified against actual code with `node risk-classify.js`
   - Round 5b → 4 remaining stale references in actionable sections. Fixed.
5. Intervention logged: attempted to skip convergence loop after Round 1. Glen corrected.
6. Glen feedback: Codex outperformed Claude on design quality for this work. Every design flaw was caught by Codex, not self-caught. Root cause: failed to test designs against actual code before submitting for review.

### Decisions
- Phase 1 plan converged (Rev 3b with final scrub). Ready for implementation.
- HIGH-approved proposal application deferred to Phase 3 (forgeable hash problem)
- HIGH AGENT.md `full_edit` rule removed from policy; mode enforcement handles create/delete
- Phases 1 and 2 partially merged (proposal-utils.js created in Phase 1)

### Handoff written
Full handoff at `docs/HANDOFF.md` with implementation order, verified code behaviour, and all design decisions.

## Task 14 S11 Transcript Parser Review
- Glen requested review of Task 14 S11 transcript parser speaker scoping in `.claude/harness/lib/transcript-parser.js` and updated tests in `.claude/harness/tests/test-transcript-parser.js`.
- Scope: verify `extractSignals` skips code fences, blockquotes, and metadata headers; correction patterns only match body text; check fence toggle correctness, nested fences, and edge cases. Required output: PASS or issues.
- Reviewed parser diff and test file. Ran `node .claude/harness/tests/test-transcript-parser.js`: 12/12 passed.
- Ran disposable probes for four-backtick outer fences containing inner three-backtick lines, indented fences, all listed metadata headers, and space-prefixed blockquotes.
- Result: issues found. Simple fences, direct blockquotes, and listed metadata headers work, but the boolean fence toggle mishandles longer outer fences with inner shorter fences, causing both false positives inside fences and false negatives after the real close. Indented fences and space-prefixed blockquotes also still match.

## Round 2 Task 14 S11 Verification
- Glen requested Round 2 verification of three reported transcript parser fixes: fence tracking stores marker length and only closes on matching-or-longer backticks, blockquote detection uses `/^\s*>/`, and metadata header checks trim whitespace.

- Ran disposable probes: four-backtick outer fence with inner three-backtick correction text emitted 0 signals; same fence followed by body correction emitted exactly 1 signal after close; indented blockquote emitted 0; space-prefixed fence emitted 0; space-prefixed metadata header emitted 0.
- Verification conclusion: PASS, no issues found in the requested probes.

## Phase 1 RHO Harness Hardening Review
- 2026-06-17 04:09 BST: Glen requested adversarial review of Phase 1 RHO harness hardening changes in `.claude/harness/lib/risk-classify.js`, `.claude/harness/config/risk-policy.json`, `.claude/harness/tests/test-risk-classify.js`, using `docs/specs/phase1-apply-gate-hardening-plan.md` and parent spec as references. Scope is bugs, logic errors, spec deviations, security holes, and test gaps with severity and line numbers.
- Loaded starting context: existing 2026-06-17 session log, live conversation context, NBI_Brain, intelligence brief, Glen working profile, receiving-code-review skill, using-superpowers skill, and senior_engineer role context.
- Review completed. Supplied classifier tests pass 30/30, but targeted probes found two substantive issues: current legacy/no-operation callers now classify `.claude/skills/**/SKILL.md` as LOW under the real policy, which combines with the existing apply-gate not passing `operation`; and unknown HIGH/BLOCKED action strings skip the higher rule and can fall through to LOW rather than failing closed. Also flagged test coverage mismatch around T-RC6/backward compatibility.

## Task 15 review - S12 bootstrap git history

- Loaded handoff/session context: existing 2026-06-17 session log, NBI_Brain, Glen working profile, intelligence brief, and senior_engineer role context.
- Starting state: Glen requested code review of `.claude/harness/lib/bootstrap.js` and `.claude/harness/tests/test-bootstrap-git.js`, focused on git-history domain inference, idempotency, harness prefix detection, and event metadata.
- Review completed: `node .claude\harness\tests\test-bootstrap-git.js` passed 7/7. Findings: harness detection is substring-based rather than prefix/scope exact, causing `feat: ... harness ...` and `docs(harness): ...` to be inferred as `harness`; git history is marked bootstrapped even when `git log` fails, preventing a later retry.
